name: Google Analytics
description: Web traffic and user behavior analytics provided by Google.
policyText: We use Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google LLC, to collect information about how visitors use our website. Google Analytics uses cookies to help us analyze usage patterns. The information generated by the cookie about your use of this website (including your IP address) will be transmitted to and stored by Google on servers in the United States. We have enabled IP anonymization so that your IP address is truncated before being transmitted. You can opt out of Google Analytics by installing the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on.

name: Plausible Analytics
description: Privacy-friendly analytics that don't use cookies or collect personal data.
policyText: We use Plausible Analytics, a privacy-focused web analytics service, to understand overall trends in how our website is used. Plausible does not use cookies, does not collect any personal data, and does not track individuals across websites. All data is aggregated and anonymized. No data is sent to or stored by any third party.

name: Mixpanel
description: Product analytics for tracking user actions within web and mobile apps.
policyText: We use Mixpanel, a product analytics service, to collect information about how you interact with our application. Mixpanel collects event data such as button clicks, page views, and feature usage. You can opt out of Mixpanel tracking by contacting us at {{contactEmail}} or by using the in-app opt-out mechanism.

name: Stripe
description: Payment processing for credit card transactions.
policyText: We use Stripe, Inc. to process payments made through our website. When you make a purchase, Stripe collects your payment information (such as credit card details, billing address, and email) directly. We do not see or store your full credit card number, expiration date, or CVV. Stripe handles this information in accordance with PCI-DSS standards.

name: PayPal
description: Online payment processor supporting PayPal balance and linked accounts.
policyText: We accept payments via PayPal. When you choose PayPal at checkout, you will be redirected to PayPal's website to authorize the payment. PayPal will share with us your name, email, and billing address, but not your full financial information.

name: LemonSqueezy
description: Merchant of record for digital product sales and subscriptions.
policyText: We use LemonSqueezy to sell and deliver digital products. LemonSqueezy acts as the Merchant of Record, which means they handle payment processing, tax collection (including VAT and sales tax), and order fulfillment. They will collect your name, email, billing address, and payment information when you make a purchase.

name: Mailchimp
description: Email newsletter delivery and audience management.
policyText: We use Mailchimp (Intuit Inc.) to manage our email newsletter and marketing communications. When you subscribe to our newsletter, your email address and any information you provide are transferred to Mailchimp for storage and email delivery. You can unsubscribe at any time using the link at the bottom of any email.

name: ConvertKit
description: Email marketing platform for creators and small businesses.
policyText: We use ConvertKit (now Kit) to send email newsletters and marketing communications. When you subscribe, your email address is stored with ConvertKit. We may track email opens and link clicks to understand engagement. You can unsubscribe at any time via the link in any email.

name: SendGrid
policyText: We use SendGrid (Twilio Inc.) to send transactional emails such as account notifications, password resets, and purchase receipts. SendGrid processes the email content, recipient address, and any metadata required for delivery.

name: Google AdSense
policyText: We use Google AdSense, a contextual advertising service provided by Google, to display ads on our website. Google AdSense uses cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to our website and other websites. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google's Ads Settings.

name: Facebook Pixel
description: Conversion tracking and audience-building for Facebook and Instagram ads.
policyText: We use the Meta Pixel (Facebook Pixel) to measure the effectiveness of our advertising and to build custom audiences for ads shown on Facebook and Instagram. The Pixel collects information about your actions on our website and shares it with Meta. You can opt out of Meta Pixel tracking by adjusting your Facebook ad preferences.

name: Google Sign-In
description: OAuth login using a Google account.
policyText: You can sign in to our service using your Google account via Google OAuth. When you choose this option, Google shares with us your name, email address, and profile picture. We do not receive your Google password. You can revoke our access at any time from your Google account settings.

name: Facebook Login
description: OAuth login using a Facebook account.
policyText: You can sign in to our service using your Facebook account. When you choose this option, Facebook shares with us your name, email address, and profile picture. We do not receive your Facebook password. You can revoke our access from your Facebook account's Apps and Websites settings.

name: YouTube Embeds
description: Embedded YouTube videos on our pages.
policyText: We embed YouTube videos on our website. When you view a page with an embedded YouTube video, YouTube (Google) may collect information about your visit, including your IP address and viewing behavior. We use YouTube's privacy-enhanced mode where available, which limits data collection until you play the video.

name: Vimeo Embeds
description: Embedded Vimeo videos on our pages.
policyText: We embed Vimeo videos on our website. When you view a page with an embedded Vimeo video, Vimeo may collect information about your visit, including your IP address and viewing behavior. Vimeo uses cookies for analytics and advertising purposes.

name: Cloudflare
description: CDN, DDoS protection, and DNS services.
policyText: We use Cloudflare to deliver our website content, provide DDoS protection, and route traffic efficiently. Cloudflare acts as an intermediary between you and our servers, and may process your IP address, request URLs, and headers to provide these services.

name: Amazon Web Services
policyText: We use Amazon Web Services (AWS) to host our website and store data. AWS processes the data necessary to provide our services on our behalf, including web requests, file storage, and database operations. AWS may store data in multiple geographic regions.
